title: Thelypodium linearifolium
taxonomy: Thelypodium linearifolium is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. It belongs to the genus Thelypodium, which is a group of plants that are native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The genus Thelypodium is further classified within the tribe Thelypodiinae. This plant is also known as the linear-leaved thelypodium, which refers to its distinctive linear-shaped leaves. As a member of the Brassicaceae family, Thelypodium linearifolium is closely related to other plants like mustard, broccoli, and cauliflower.

The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of conditions such as arthritis and digestive issues.
